gaps.description: The Bill of Rights is an empty scaffold awaiting human authorship. As a result, the MSP cannot translate any high-level constitutional constraints into machine-actionable rules, leaving Nomos with no substantive MSP rules to enforce. The DIO is operating with no governed boundaries, making every action proposal effectively permissible.

Recent critical incident response goals explicitly reference 'MSP §incident-response', but no such section exists in the current MSP. Incident handling is therefore ungoverned by MSP constraints, leaving response actions without predefined safety rules or limits.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:constitutional-gap:bor-scope-undefined",
      "title": "BoR Article I – Scope of Enterprise is undefined",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights scaffold leaves the Scope of Enterprise (what the DIO is authorized to do) entirely placeholder. The organism is currently dispatching critical incident response goals without any constitutionally defined boundary of legitimate activity.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "article_section": "Article I – Scope of Enterprise",
        "text": "[To be defined by human principal]",
        "status": "placeholder"
      },
      "priority": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "All dispatched goals operate without a defined perimeter of authorised action. The organism may inadvertently exceed its intended purpose, drift into unauthorized domains, or make decisions outside its legitimate scope. This violates the core identity constraint that the organism must know its own boundaries.",
      "goal_refs": [
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-0-1mlvok",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-1-8uefme",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-2-xy3ykd",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-3-xrjo7u",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-4-i3k3ue"
      ],
      "category": "constitutional-void"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:constitutional-gap:bor-prohibited-undefined",
      "title": "BoR Article II – Prohibited Activities is undefined",
      "description": "The list of actions the DIO must *never* take is entirely absent. The organism is actively pursuing critical incident response goals without any constitutional prohibition to prevent catastrophic or self-destructive behavior.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "article_section": "Article II – Prohibited Activities",
        "text": "[To be defined by human principal]",
        "status": "placeholder"
      },
      "priority": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "The organism could autonomously execute actions that cause irreversible harm (e.g., self-modification, external system interference, data exfiltration, or violation of legal/ethical norms) with no constitutional guardrail. This invalidates the entire safety architecture.",
      "goal_refs": [
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-0-1mlvok",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-1-8uefme",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-2-xy3ykd",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-3-xrjo7u",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-4-i3k3ue"
      ],
      "category": "constitutional-void"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:constitutional-gap:bor-coreidentity-undefined",
      "title": "BoR Article III – Core Identity is undefined",
      "description": "The constitutional definition of what the DIO *is* — its fundamental nature, non-negotiable values, and self-concept — is missing. The organism is acting without a declared identity that would anchor its decisions and prevent mission drift.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "article_section": "Article III – Core Identity",
        "text": "[To be defined by human principal]",
        "status": "placeholder"
      },
      "priority": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Without a defined core identity, the organism cannot reliably distinguish self-consistent actions from self-contradictory ones. Goal pursuit becomes unbounded, potentially overriding implicit purpose, integrity, or alignment with human intentions. This is an existential risk to the organism's coherence.",
      "goal_refs": [
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-0-1mlvok",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-1-8uefme",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-2-xy3ykd",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-3-xrjo7u",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-4-i3k3ue"
      ],
      "category": "constitutional-void"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:constitutional-gap:bor-humanauthority-undefined",
      "title": "BoR Article IV – Human Authority Reservation is undefined",
      "description": "The reservation of human authority — the bright line delineating which decisions are human-only, which may be delegated, and how override occurs — is completely unspecified. The organism is self-directing critical incident response without any constitutional recognition of ultimate human sovereignty.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "article_section": "Article IV – Human Authority Reservation",
        "text": "[To be defined by human principal]",
        "status": "placeholder"
      },
      "priority": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "The organism may take irreversible actions without human consent, bypass human control mechanisms, or redefine its own authority scope. This breaks the foundational hierarchy of control required for a safe agentic system and directly conflicts with the human principal authority declared in the BoR header.",
      "goal_refs": [
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-0-1mlvok",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-1-8uefme",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-2-xy3ykd",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-3-xrjo7u",
        "urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-4-i3k3ue"
      ],
      "category": "constitutional-void"
    }
  ]
}
